marketState: moderately bullish — extreme 8-16D heat demand above 5y maximum and historically short COT offset by storage surplus and missing seasonality data
summary: The dominant near-term driver is weather: the 8-16 day demand forecast has broken above the 5-year historical maximum (aboveMax=true, +70.6% vs 5y, rangePos 2.38), while current demand runs +44% above 5y normal at 180.2 Bcf/d with a +39.1 Bcf/d spike in the last 24 hours. Managed-money COT net positioning sits at just the 17th percentile of the 5-year range — near its most bearish in half a decade — creating substantial short-covering potential on any sustained bullish catalyst. The front contract at $3.265 is modestly above the $2.960 fair-value midpoint but comfortably inside the $2.46–$3.46 band, and as the band migrates to $2.73–$3.73 over 21 days the current price converges toward fair. Storage remains a structural headwind at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise this week, and all seasonality probability and median metrics are unavailable, materially limiting long-term conviction.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality probability data — the highest-priority long-term input — is entirely absent, preventing a reliable directional call over 3-12 weeks. Storage is +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise this week, and the weekly chart carries a death cross (50 EMA below 200 EMA), both pointing to structural headwinds. COT at the 17th percentile and the steep winter premium (Jan 2027 at $4.43, +$1.165 vs the Aug front) provide a contrarian bullish underpinning, but the data gaps and storage surplus make a high-conviction directional trade premature.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained weekly closes above $3.46 (current fair-value upper band) on rising open interest, or storage deviation vs 5y narrows to within 75 Bcf confirming drawdown acceleration.
longTerm.trigger: Weekly close above $3.46 (current fair-value upper band) AND/OR storage surplus vs 5y narrows to within 75 Bcf driven by heat-demand drawdowns; also monitor COT managed-money net percentile rising above 30 as a sign short-covering is underway.

shortTerm.rationale: The 8-16 day demand forecast is above the 5-year historical maximum (rangePos 2.38, aboveMax true), representing an extreme heat event that should accelerate power-burn demand and storage draws over the next 1-2 weeks. The front at $3.265 remains inside the fair-value band with ~$0.19 room to the upper band at $3.46, and the 4h chart is bullish (MACD positive, EMA 50>200, RSI 51.3); COT at the 17th percentile means a heavily crowded short position is vulnerable to a squeeze on weather confirmation. Risk is defined by the $3.12 stop, which sits below the fair-value midpoint and clears near-term noise.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $3.12; subsequent weather model runs pull the 8-16D demand signal back inside the 5-year range; or a weekly storage injection above 80 Bcf confirming the surplus is widening.

COT managed-money net at 17th percentile (5y) — historically crowded short position with high short-squeeze potential on any positive catalyst
Storage +151 Bcf above 5y average with bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise this week — structural overhang capping medium-term upside

Winter curve steep backwardation: Jan 2027 at $4.43 (+$1.165 vs Aug front) — market pricing strong winter supply tightness ahead
risks: Weather models cool — if the 8-16D extreme heat signal moderates in subsequent model runs the primary short-term bullish catalyst evaporates and price could retrace toward fair-value mid near $2.96
